body.title: Wire cc_queue.py to call Scraps before add/claim transitions
body.result: wired _scraps_validate() into cmd_add and cmd_claim; 9 new tests; fixed worker/foreman seed tickets; graceful offline degradation; commits adb17bf (agent_datacenter) + 10549f81 (TheIgors)
body.description:
Scraps device exists but doesn't intercept queue state changes yet — tickets can still be filed and claimed without validation.

In cc_queue.py `cmd_add` and `cmd_claim`, call `scraps_validate_ticket(ticket_json)` before proceeding. If Scraps returns invalid: print the issue list and abort the transition. If Scraps is offline: print 'Scraps offline — validation skipped' and continue (never hard-block on device offline). On pass: Scraps sign-off is written to ticket metadata by the device.

**Affected files:** lab/claudecode/cc_queue.py (cmd_add and cmd_claim — pre-flight calls), devices/scraps/scraps_device.py (called)
**Design rules:** graceful degradation is a hard requirement — Scraps offline must never block the queue; only an explicit invalid verdict blocks; no SQLite
**Scope boundary:** In scope: gate at add and claim transitions; display issue list on block; write sign-off on pass. Out of scope: setstatus/done/close transitions (V2), retroactive scan of existing tickets, UI changes.
**Test plan:** test add with empty-body ticket returns error with Scraps issue list; test valid ticket passes and metadata contains scraps_validated; test Scraps offline (mock device unreachable) → warning printed + transition proceeds; test claim on already-validated ticket skips redundant Scraps call
